name: Sage Dressing
recipeIngredient: 16 slices White bread, dried - broken into small pieces
1 teaspoon Freshly ground black pepper (or to taste)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put 4 tablespoons melted butter in a skillet. Add onions and celery and cook, stirring often, over moderate heat until the vegetables are soft but not browned.
2. In a large bowl, combine dried bread, sage, parsley, salt, and pepper.
3. Add onion and celery mixture and remaining melted butter to the bowl. Toss until well mixed (using your hands works best for mixing).
4. Gradually add broth, a little at a time, while tossing the mixture to moisten thoroughly. Take care not to add too much liquid; the stuffing should not become soggy.
5. To test the seasoning of the stuffing, melt a small amount of butter in a skillet and add a rounded tablespoon of stuffing. Stir until lightly golden. If needed, adjust flavor by adding more sage, onions, or celery. Makes enough for 16-pound turkey.
